content: Labels and Selectors
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. We have deployed a number of PODs. They are labelled with tier, env and bu. How many PODs exist in the dev environment (env)? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods --selector env=dev NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE app-1-5hskg 1/1 Running 0 71s app-1-8m5gl 1/1 Running 0 71s db-1-jfd5z 1/1 Running 0 70s app-1-jnd7g 1/1 Running 0 71s db-1-54kdk 1/1 Running 0 70s db-1-xcxv7 1/1 Running 0 70s db-1-2tffc 1/1 Running 0 70s OR kubectl get pods --selector env=dev --no-headers | wc -l A. 7
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. How many PODs are in the finance business unit (bu)? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pod --selector bu=finance NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE app-1-5hskg 1/1 Running 0 2m13s app-1-8m5gl 1/1 Running 0 2m13s db-2-mp75n 1/1 Running 0 2m12s auth 1/1 Running 0 2m12s app-1-jnd7g 1/1 Running 0 2m13s app-1-zzxdf 1/1 Running 0 2m12s OR kubectl get pods --selector bu=finance --no-headers | wc -l A. 6
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. How many objects are in the prod environment including PODs, ReplicaSets and any other objects? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get all --selector env=prod NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE pod/db-2-zkq5x 1/1 Running 0 9m9s pod/auth 1/1 Running 0 9m9s pod/app-1-zzxdf 1/1 Running 0 9m9s pod/app-2-cjp6w 1/1 Running 0 9m9s NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE service/app-1 ClusterIP 10.43.116.128 <none> 3306/TCP 9m9s NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE replicaset.apps/db-2 1 1 1 9m9s replicaset.apps/app-2 1 1 1 9m9s OR controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get all --selector env=prod --no-headers | wc -l 7 A. 7
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Identify the POD which is part of the prod environment, the finance BU and of frontend tier? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods --selector env=prod,bu=finance,tier=frontend ontend NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE pod/app-1-zzxdf 1/1 Running 0 11m A. app-1-zzxdf
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. A ReplicaSet definition file is given replicaset-definition-1.yaml. Attempt to create the replicaset; you will encounter an issue with the file. Try to fix it. Once you fix the issue, create the replicaset from the definition file. controlplane ~ вһң vim replicaset-definition-1.yaml --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: name: replicaset-1 spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: tier: front-end template: metadata: labels: tier: nginx(X) -> front-end(O) spec: containers: - name: nginx image: nginx controlplane ~ вһң kubectl apply -f replicaset-definition-1.yaml replicaset.apps/replicaset-1 created
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Do any taints exist on node01 node? controlplane ~ вң– kubectl describe node node01 Name: node01 Roles: <none> Labels: beta.kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 beta.kubernetes.io/os=linux kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 kubernetes.io/hostname=node01 kubernetes.io/os=linux Annotations: flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-data: {"VNI":1,"VtepMAC":"72:10:2d:a2:75:8d"} flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-type: vxlan flannel.alpha.coreos.com/kube-subnet-manager: true flannel.alpha.coreos.com/public-ip: 192.14.218.6 kubeadm.alpha.kubernetes.io/cri-socket: unix:///var/run/containerd/containerd.sock node.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: 0 volumes.kubernetes.io/controller-managed-attach-detach: true CreationTimestamp: Mon, 17 Jun 2024 08:28:32 +0000 Taints: <none> ... A. None
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Create a taint on node01 with key of spray, value of mortein and effect of NoSchedule controlplane ~ вһң kubectl taint nodes node01 spray=mortein:NoSchedule node/node01 tainted controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe node node01 Name: node01 Roles: <none> Labels: beta.kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 beta.kubernetes.io/os=linux kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 kubernetes.io/hostname=node01 kubernetes.io/os=linux Annotations: flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-data: {"VNI":1,"VtepMAC":"72:10:2d:a2:75:8d"} flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-type: vxlan flannel.alpha.coreos.com/kube-subnet-manager: true flannel.alpha.coreos.com/public-ip: 192.14.218.6 kubeadm.alpha.kubernetes.io/cri-socket: unix:///var/run/containerd/containerd.sock node.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: 0 volumes.kubernetes.io/controller-managed-attach-detach: true CreationTimestamp: Mon, 17 Jun 2024 08:28:32 +0000 Taints: spray=mortein:NoSchedule ...
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Create another pod names bee with the nginx image, which has a toleration set to the taint mortein. Image name: nginx Key: spray Value: mortein Effects: NoSchedule Status: Running controlplane ~ вһң vim bee-pod.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: bee spec: containers: - image: nginx name: bee tolerations: - key: spray value: mortein effect: NoSchedule operator: Equal :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f bee-pod.yaml
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Do you see any taints on controlplane node? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe node controlplane Name: controlplane Roles: control-plane Labels: beta.kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 beta.kubernetes.io/os=linux kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 kubernetes.io/hostname=controlplane kubernetes.io/os=linux node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane= node.kubernetes.io/exclude-from-external-load-balancers= Annotations: flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-data: {"VNI":1,"VtepMAC":"aa:0d:ad:10:90:1d"} flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-type: vxlan flannel.alpha.coreos.com/kube-subnet-manager: true flannel.alpha.coreos.com/public-ip: 192.14.218.3 kubeadm.alpha.kubernetes.io/cri-socket: unix:///var/run/containerd/containerd.sock node.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: 0 volumes.kubernetes.io/controller-managed-attach-detach: true CreationTimestamp: Mon, 17 Jun 2024 08:27:49 +0000 Taints: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule ...
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Remove the taint on controlplane, which currently has the taint effect of NoSchedule. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl taint nodes controlplane node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule- node/controlplane untainted controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe node controlplane Name: controlplane Roles: control-plane Labels: beta.kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 beta.kubernetes.io/os=linux kubernetes.io/arch=amd64 kubernetes.io/hostname=controlplane kubernetes.io/os=linux node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane= node.kubernetes.io/exclude-from-external-load-balancers= Annotations: flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-data: {"VNI":1,"VtepMAC":"aa:0d:ad:10:90:1d"} flannel.alpha.coreos.com/backend-type: vxlan flannel.alpha.coreos.com/kube-subnet-manager: true flannel.alpha.coreos.com/public-ip: 192.14.218.3 kubeadm.alpha.kubernetes.io/cri-socket: unix:///var/run/containerd/containerd.sock node.alpha.kubernetes.io/ttl: 0 volumes.kubernetes.io/controller-managed-attach-detach: true CreationTimestamp: Mon, 17 Jun 2024 08:27:49 +0000 Taints: <none>

content: Q. Set Node Affinity to the deployment to place the pods on node01 only. controlplane ~ вң– kubectl edit deployment blue --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: blue spec: replicas: 3 selector: matchLabels: run: nginx template: metadata: labels: run: nginx spec: containers: - image: nginx imagePullPolicy: Always name: nginx affinity: nodeAffinity: requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution: nodeSelectorTerms: - matchExpressions: - key: color operator: In values: - blue :wq deployment.apps/blue edited
title: Kubernetes Scheduling

content: Q. Create a new deployment named red with the nginx image and 2 replicas, and ensure it gets placed on the controlplane node only. Use the label key - node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane - which is already set on the controlplane node. controlplane ~ вң– vim red-deploy.yaml --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: red spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: run: nginx template: metadata: labels: run: nginx spec: containers: - image: nginx imagePullPolicy: Always name: nginx affinity: nodeAffinity: requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution: nodeSelectorTerms: - matchExpressions: - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane operator: Exists :wq controlplane ~ вң– kubectl create -f red-deploy.yaml deployment.apps/red created

content: Voting-app Pod, Service мғқм„ұ {seilylook} рҹҡҖ cd pods {seilylook} рҹҡҖ kubectl create -f voting-app-pod.yaml {seilylook} рҹҡҖ cd .. {seilylook} рҹҡҖ cd services {seilylook} рҹҡҖ kubectl create -f voting-app-service.yaml Pod, Service мғқм„ұ нҷ•мқё {seilylook} рҹҡҖ kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE voting-app-pod 1/1 Running 0 9m1s {seilylook} рҹҡҖ kubectl get services NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE kubernetes ClusterIP 10.96.0.1 <none> 443/TCP 86m voting-service NodePort 10.100.147.17 <none> 80:30004/TCP 8m49s URL нҷ•мқё {seilylook} рҹҡҖ minikube service voting-service --url http://127.0.0.1:51966 вқ— darwin м—җм„ң Docker л“ңлқјмқҙлІ„лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкі  мһҲкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ, н„°лҜёл„җмқ„ м—ҙм–ҙм•ј мӢӨн–үн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ Warning voting-serviceмҷҖ result-serviceм—җ м§Җм •н•ң portмқё 30004, 30005к°Җ м•„лӢҢ лӢӨлҘё нҸ¬нҠё лІҲнҳёк°Җ м¶ңл ҘлҗҳлҠ” кІғмқҖ minikubeм—җм„ң мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң лӢӨлҘё нҸ¬нҠёлЎң л§Өн•‘н•ҳлҠ” кІғмқҙкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ мӢ кІҪм“°м§Җ м•Ҡм•„лҸ„ лҗңлӢӨ.

content: Q. What is the image used to create the new pods? You must look at one of the new pods in detail to figure this out. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx 1/1 Running 0 4m23s newpods-dn5jz 1/1 Running 0 4m5s newpods-bj2zg 1/1 Running 0 4m5s newpods-m9j8x 1/1 Running 0 4m5s controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe pod newpods-dn5jz Name: newpods-dn5jz Namespace: default Priority: 0 Service Account: default content: Q. What does the READY column in the output of the kubectl get pods command indicate? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx 1/1 Running 0 13m newpods-dn5jz 1/1 Running 0 13m newpods-bj2zg 1/1 Running 0 13m newpods-m9j8x 1/1 Running 0 13m webapp 1/2 ImagePullBackOff 0 6m58s A. Running Containers in POD / Total Containers in POD
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Delete the webapp Pod. Once deleted, wait for the pod to fully terminate. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl delete pod webapp pod "webapp" deleted
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new pod with the name redis and the image redis123. Use a pod-definition YAML file. And yes the image name is wrong! controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx 1/1 Running 0 17m newpods-m9j8x 1/1 Running 1 (10s ago) 16m newpods-bj2zg 1/1 Running 1 (10s ago) 16m newpods-dn5jz 1/1 Running 1 (10s ago) 16m controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run redis --image=redis123 --dry-run=client -o yaml > redis-definition.yaml controlplane ~ вһң ls redis-definition.yaml sample.yaml controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f redis-definition.yaml pod/redis created controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx 1/1 Running 0 18m newpods-m9j8x 1/1 Running 1 (64s ago) 17m newpods-bj2zg 1/1 Running 1 (64s ago) 17m newpods-dn5jz 1/1 Running 1 (64s ago) 17m redis 0/1 ErrImagePull 0 6s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Now change the image on this pod to redis. Once done, the pod should be in a running state. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit pod redis apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: run: redis name: redis spec: containers: - image: redis name: redis resources: {} dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always status: {} ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl apply -f redis-definition.yaml controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx 1/1 Running 0 20m newpods-m9j8x 1/1 Running 1 (3m58s ago) 20m newpods-bj2zg 1/1 Running 1 (3m58s ago) 20m newpods-dn5jz 1/1 Running 1 (3m58s ago) 20m redis 1/1 Running 0 3m Tip kubectl edit лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙ redis PODмқҳ image ліҖкІҪн•ҳкё° kubectl edit pod redis Vi | Nano лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј нҶөн•ң нҺём§‘кё°лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙ мқҙлҜём§ҖлҘј redis123м—җм„ң redisлЎң ліҖкІҪн–ҲлӢӨл©ҙ, kubectl apploy лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҙ мқҙлҜём§ҖлҘј м—…лҚ°мқҙнҠё н•ҙмӨҖлӢӨ. kubectl apply -f redis-definition.yaml
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Replica Set
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. How many replica sets? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 5s A. 1
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. How many PODs are DESIRED in the new-replica-set? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 5s A. 4
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. What is the image used to create the pods in the new-replica-set? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe rs new-replica-set Name: new-replica-set Namespace: default Selector: name=busybox-pod Labels: <none> Annotations: <none> Replicas: 4 current / 4 desired Pods Status: 0 Running / 4 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Pod Template: Labels: name=busybox-pod Containers: busybox-container: Image: busybox777 Port: <none> Host Port: <none> Command: sh -c echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 Environment: <none> Mounts: <none> Volumes: <none> Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m41s replicaset-controller Created pod: new-replica-set-4jm4s Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m41s replicaset-controller Created pod: new-replica-set-8pj5l Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m41s replicaset-controller Created pod: new-replica-set-7gbl5 Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m41s replicaset-controller Created pod: new-replica-set-2jcnr A. busybox777
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. How many PODs are READY in the new-replica-set? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 4m30s A. 0
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Why do you think the PODs are not ready? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 4m30s A. The image BUSYBOX777 does not exist
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Delete any one of the 4 PODs controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 4m30s controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE new-replica-set-7gbl5 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 6m new-replica-set-4jm4s 0/1 ErrImagePull 0 6m new-replica-set-8pj5l 0/1 ErrImagePull 0 6m new-replica-set-2jcnr 0/1 ErrImagePull 0 6m controlplane ~ вһң kubectl delete pod new-replica-set-7gbl5 pod "new-replica-set-7gbl5" deleted controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE new-replica-set-4jm4s 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 6m39s new-replica-set-8pj5l 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 6m39s new-replica-set-2jcnr 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 6m39s new-replica-set-f2p2k 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 27s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Why are there still 4 PODs, even after you deleted one? A. ReplicaSet ensures that desired number of PODs always run
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a ReplicaSet using the replicaset-definition-1.yaml file located at /root/. There is an issue with the file, so try to fix it. controlplane ~ вһң vim replicaset-definition-1.yaml apiVersion: v1 (x) -> apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: name: replicaset-1 spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: tier: frontend template: metadata: labels: tier: frontend spec: containers: - name: nginx image: nginx ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f replicaset-definition-1.yaml replicaset.apps/replicaset-1 created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Fix the issue in the replicaset-definition-2.yaml file and create a ReplicaSet using it. This file is located at /root/. controlplane ~ вһң vim replicaset-definition-2.yaml apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: name: replicaset-2 spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: tier: nginx template: metadata: labels: tier: nginx spec: containers: - name: nginx image: nginx ~ ~ ~ :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f replicaset-definition-2.yaml replicaset.apps/replicaset-2 created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Delete the two newly created ReplicaSets - replicaset-1 and replicaset-2 controlplane ~ вң– kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 22m replicaset-1 2 2 2 11m replicaset-2 2 2 2 41s controlplane ~ вһң kubectl delete rs replicaset-1 replicaset.apps "replicaset-1" deleted controlplane ~ вһң kubectl delete rs replicaset-2 replicaset.apps "replicaset-2" deleted controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 4 4 0 23m
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Fix the original replica set new-replica-set to use the correct busybox image. Either delete and recreate the ReplicaSet or Update the existing ReplicaSet and then delete all PODs, so new ones with the correct image will be created. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit rs new-replica-set apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T05:57:22Z" generation: 1 name: new-replica-set namespace: default resourceVersion: "1130" uid: 1e1c0ce5-d403-4bae-b53f-3b18ab954e2a spec: replicas: 4 selector: matchLabels: name: busybox-pod template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: busybox-pod spec: containers: - command: - sh - -c - echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 image: busybox imagePullPolicy: Always name: busybox-container resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30 status: fullyLabeledReplicas: 4 observedGeneration: 1 replicas: 4 ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq replicaset.apps/new-replica-set edited controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE new-replica-set-4hj6z 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-2fjtt 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-5h2xp 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-rvglt 1/1 Running 0 5s controlplane ~ вһң kubectl delete pod new-replica-set-4hj6z controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE new-replica-set-7fsds 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-2fjtt 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-5h2xp 1/1 Running 0 5s new-replica-set-rvglt 1/1 Running 0 5s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Scale the ReplicaSet to 5 PODs. Use kubectl scale command or edit the replicaset using kubectl edit replicaset. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit rs new-replica-set apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T05:57:22Z" generation: 1 name: new-replica-set namespace: default resourceVersion: "1130" uid: 1e1c0ce5-d403-4bae-b53f-3b18ab954e2a spec: replicas: 5 selector: matchLabels: name: busybox-pod template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: busybox-pod spec: containers: - command: - sh - -c - echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 image: busybox imagePullPolicy: Always name: busybox-container resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30 status: fullyLabeledReplicas: 4 observedGeneration: 1 replicas: 4 ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq replicaset.apps/new-replica-set edited controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 5 5 5 2m57s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Now scale the ReplicaSet down to 2 PODs. Use the kubectl scale command or edit the replicaset using kubectl edit replicaset. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit rs new-replica-set apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T05:57:22Z" generation: 1 name: new-replica-set namespace: default resourceVersion: "1130" uid: 1e1c0ce5-d403-4bae-b53f-3b18ab954e2a spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: name: busybox-pod template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: busybox-pod spec: containers: - command: - sh - -c - echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 image: busybox imagePullPolicy: Always name: busybox-container resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30 status: fullyLabeledReplicas: 4 observedGeneration: 1 replicas: 4 ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq replicaset.apps/new-replica-set edited controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE new-replica-set 2 2 2 5m17s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Deployment
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. How many Deployments exist on the system? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get deployments NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE frontend-deployment 0/4 4 0 9s A. 1

content: Q. How many ReplicaSets exist on the system now? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get rs NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE frontend-deployment-7b9984b987 4 4 0 55s A. 1
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. How many PODs exist on the system? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE frontend-deployment-7b9984b987-v58w9 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 96s frontend-deployment-7b9984b987-jrmbt 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 96s frontend-deployment-7b9984b987-hpxnn 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 96s frontend-deployment-7b9984b987-rth5d 0/1 ImagePullBackOff 0 96s A. 4

content: Q. What is the image used to create the pods in the new deployment? controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe deployment frontend-deployment Name: frontend-deployment Namespace: default CreationTimestamp: Tue, 11 Jun 2024 06:55:55 +0000 Labels: <none> Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 1 Selector: name=busybox-pod Replicas: 4 desired | 4 updated | 4 total | 0 available | 4 unavailable StrategyType: RollingUpdate MinReadySeconds: 0 RollingUpdateStrategy: 25% max unavailable, 25% max surge Pod Template: Labels: name=busybox-pod Containers: busybox-container: Image: busybox888 Port: <none> Host Port: <none> Command: sh -c echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 Environment: <none> Mounts: <none> Volumes: <none> Conditions: Type Status Reason ---- ------ ------ Available False MinimumReplicasUnavailable Progressing True ReplicaSetUpdated OldReplicaSets: <none> NewReplicaSet: frontend-deployment-7b9984b987 (4/4 replicas created) Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal ScalingReplicaSet 2m56s deployment-controller Scaled up replica set frontend-deployment-7b9984b987 to 4 A. BUSYBOX888
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new Deployment using the deployment-definition-1.yaml file located at /root/. There is an issue with the file, so try to fix it. controlplane ~ вһң vim deployment-definition-1.yaml --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: deployment-1 spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: name: busybox-pod template: metadata: labels: name: busybox-pod spec: containers: - name: busybox-container image: busybox888 command: - sh - "-c" - echo Hello Kubernetes! && sleep 3600 ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f deployment-definition-1.yaml deployment.apps/deployment-1 created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new Deployment with the below attributes using your own deployment definition file. Name: httpd-frontend; Replicas: 3; Image: httpd:2.4-alpine controlplane ~ вһң vim deployment-definition-httpd.yaml --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: httpd-frontend spec: replicas: 3 selector: matchLabels: name: httpd-frontend template: metadata: labels: name: httpd-frontend spec: containers: - name: httpd-frontend image: httpd:2.4-alpine ~ ~ ~ ~ :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f deployment-definition-httpd.yaml deployment.apps/httpd-frontend created controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get deployments NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE frontend-deployment 0/4 4 0 9m23s deployment-1 0/2 2 0 3m15s httpd-frontend 3/3 3 3 17s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Rolling Updates and Rollback
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Inspect the deployment and identify the current strategy controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe deployment Name: frontend Namespace: default CreationTimestamp: Tue, 11 Jun 2024 07:17:28 +0000 Labels: <none> Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 1 Selector: name=webapp Replicas: 4 desired | 4 updated | 4 total | 4 available | 0 unavailable StrategyType: RollingUpdate MinReadySeconds: 20 RollingUpdateStrategy: 25% max unavailable, 25% max surge Pod Template: Labels: name=webapp Containers: simple-webapp: Image: kodekloud/webapp-color:v1 Port: 8080/TCP Host Port: 0/TCP Environment: <none> Mounts: <none> Volumes: <none> Conditions: Type Status Reason ---- ------ ------ Available True MinimumReplicasAvailable Progressing True NewReplicaSetAvailable OldReplicaSets: <none> NewReplicaSet: frontend-685dfcc44 (4/4 replicas created) Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal ScalingReplicaSet 101s deployment-controller Scaled up replica set frontend-685dfcc44 to 4 A. RollingUpdate
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. If you were to upgrade the application now what would happen? A. PODs are upgraded few at a time
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Let us try that. Upgrade the application by setting the image on the deployment to kodekloud/webapp-color:v2. Do not delete and re-create the deployment. Only set the new image name for the existing deployment. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit deployment frontend # Please edit the object below. Lines beginning with a '#' will b e ignored, # and an empty file will abort the edit. If an error occurs while saving this file will be # reopened with the relevant failures. # apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1" creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T07:17:28Z" generation: 1 name: frontend namespace: default resourceVersion: "819" uid: d8caa22d-e29d-4c2f-b896-9436b3245d0a spec: minReadySeconds: 20 progressDeadlineSeconds: 600 replicas: 4 revisionHistoryLimit: 10 selector: matchLabels: name: webapp strategy: rollingUpdate: maxSurge: 25% maxUnavailable: 25% type: RollingUpdate template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: webapp spec: containers: - image: kodekloud/webapp-color:v2 imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent name: simple-webapp ports: - containerPort: 8080 protocol: TCP resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} :wq deployment.apps/frontend edited
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Up to how many PODs can be down for upgrade at a time Consider the current strategy settings and number of PODs - 4 controlplane ~ вһң kubectl describe deployment Name: frontend Namespace: default CreationTimestamp: Tue, 11 Jun 2024 07:17:28 +0000 Labels: <none> Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 1 Selector: name=webapp Replicas: 4 desired | 4 updated | 4 total | 4 available | 0 unavailable StrategyType: RollingUpdate MinReadySeconds: 20 RollingUpdateStrategy: 25% max unavailable, 25% max surge Pod Template: Labels: name=webapp Containers: simple-webapp: Image: kodekloud/webapp-color:v1 Port: 8080/TCP Host Port: 0/TCP Environment: <none> Mounts: <none> Volumes: <none> Conditions: Type Status Reason ---- ------ ------ Available True MinimumReplicasAvailable Progressing True NewReplicaSetAvailable OldReplicaSets: <none> NewReplicaSet: frontend-685dfcc44 (4/4 replicas created) Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal ScalingReplicaSet 101s deployment-controller Scaled up replica set frontend-685dfcc44 to 4 A. Look at the Max Unavailable value under RollingUpdateStrategy in deployment details -> мҙқ 4к°ңмқҳ POD мӨ‘мқҳ 25%лӢҲк№Ң 1
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Change the deployment strategy to Recreate. Delete and re-create the deployment if necessary. Only update the strategy type for the existing deployment. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl edit deployment frontend apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "2" creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T07:17:28Z" generation: 2 name: frontend namespace: default resourceVersion: "1074" uid: d8caa22d-e29d-4c2f-b896-9436b3245d0a spec: minReadySeconds: 20 progressDeadlineSeconds: 600 replicas: 4 revisionHistoryLimit: 10 selector: matchLabels: name: webapp strategy: type: Recreate template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: webapp spec: containers: - image: kodekloud/webapp-color:v2 imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent name: simple-webapp ports: - containerPort: 8080 protocol: TCP resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30 status: availableReplicas: 4 :wq deployment.apps/frontend edited
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Upgrade the application by setting the image on the deployment to kodekloud/webapp-color:v3. apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "2" creationTimestamp: "2024-06-11T07:17:28Z" generation: 3 name: frontend namespace: default resourceVersion: "1150" uid: d8caa22d-e29d-4c2f-b896-9436b3245d0a spec: minReadySeconds: 20 progressDeadlineSeconds: 600 replicas: 4 revisionHistoryLimit: 10 selector: matchLabels: name: webapp strategy: type: Recreate template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: name: webapp spec: containers: - image: kodekloud/webapp-color:v3 imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent name: simple-webapp ports: - containerPort: 8080 protocol: TCP resources: {} terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log terminationMessagePolicy: File dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always schedulerName: default-scheduler securityContext: {} terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30 status: availableReplicas: 4 :wq deployment.apps/frontend edited

content: Q. Create a new service to access the web application using the service-definition-1.yaml file. Name: webapp-service Type: NodePort targetPort: 8080 port: 8080 nodePort: 30080 selector: name: simple-webapp controlplane ~ вһң vim service-definition-1.yaml apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: webapp-service namespace: default spec: ports: - nodePort: 30080 port: 8080 targetPort: 8080 selector: name: simple-webapp type: NodePort :wq controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f service-definition-1.yaml service/webapp-service created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Imperative Commands
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Deploy a pod named nginx-pod using the nginx:alpine image. Use imperative commands only. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run nginx-pod --image=nginx:alpine pod/nginx-pod created controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx-pod 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 7s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Deploy a redis pod using the redis:alpine image with the labels set to tier=db. Either use imperative commands to create the pod with the labels. Or else use imperative commands to generate the pod definition file, then add the labels before creating the pod using the file. Solution 1. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run redis --image=redis:alpine --dry-run=client -oyaml > redis-pod.yaml controlplane ~ вһң ls redis-pod.yaml sample.yaml controlplane ~ вһң vim redis-pod.yaml --- apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: labels: tier: db name: redis spec: containers: - image: redis:alpine name: redis dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst restartPolicy: Always controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create -f redis-pod.yaml pod/redis created Solution 2. kubectl run redis -l tier=db --image=redis:alpine
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a service redis-service to expose the redis application within the cluster on port 6379. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl expose pod redis --port=6379 --name redis-service service/redis-service exposed controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get all NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE pod/nginx-pod 1/1 Running 0 10m pod/redis 1/1 Running 0 3m54s NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE service/kubernetes ClusterIP 10.43.0.1 <none> 443/TCP 32m service/redis-service ClusterIP 10.43.163.218 <none> 6379/TCP 18s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a deployment named webapp using the image kodekloud/webapp-color with 3 replicas. Try to use imperative commands only. Do not create definition files. controlplane ~ вң– kubectl create deployment webapp --image=kodekloud/web app-color --replicas=3 deployment.apps/webapp created controlplane ~ вһң kubectl get deployments NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE webapp 3/3 3 3 46s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new pod called custom-nginx using the nginx image and run it on container port 8080. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run custom-nginx --image=nginx --port=8080 pod/custom-nginx created controlplane ~ вң– kubectl get pods NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE nginx-pod 1/1 Running 0 18m redis 1/1 Running 0 11m webapp-799f6b587f-62vgv 1/1 Running 0 3m44s webapp-799f6b587f-c2xtk 1/1 Running 0 3m44s webapp-799f6b587f-88tbr 1/1 Running 0 3m44s custom-nginx 1/1 Running 0 32s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new namespace called dev-ns. Use imperative commands. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create namespace dev-ns namespace/dev-ns created controlplane ~ вң– kubectl get ns NAME STATUS AGE kube-system Active 42m kube-public Active 42m kube-node-lease Active 42m default Active 42m dev-ns Active 13s
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a new deployment called redis-deploy in the dev-ns namespace with the redis image. It should have 2 replicas. Use imperative commands. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl create deployment redis-deploy --image=redis --replicas=2 -n dev-ns deployment.apps/redis-deploy created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a pod called httpd using the image httpd:alpine in the default namespace. Next, create a service of type ClusterIP by the same name (httpd). The target port for the service should be 80. Try to do this with as few steps as possible. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run httpd --image=httpd:apline --port=80 --expose service/httpd created pod/httpd created
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Q. Create a pod called httpd using the image httpd:alpine in the default namespace. Next, create a service of type ClusterIP by the same name (httpd). The target port for the service should be 80. Try to do this with as few steps as possible. controlplane ~ вһң kubectl run httpd --image=httpd:alpine --port=80 --expose service/httpd created pod/httpd created Info
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: Difference of kubectl create vs run kubectlмқҖ Kubernetes нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„°мҷҖ мғҒнҳё мһ‘мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” лҚ° мӮ¬мҡ©лҗҳлҠ” м»Өл§Ёл“ң лқјмқё лҸ„кө¬мқҙлӢӨ. kubectl createмҷҖ kubectl runмқҖ лӘЁл‘җ Kubernetes лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” лҚ° мӮ¬мҡ©лҗҳм§Җл§Ң, к°Ғк°Ғмқҳ лӘ©м Ғкіј кё°лҠҘмқҙ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ. Kubectl create kubectl create лӘ…л №м–ҙлҠ” лӘ…мӢңм ҒмңјлЎң лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ м •мқҳ нҢҢмқјмқ„ кё°л°ҳмңјлЎң Kubernetes лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•ңлӢӨ. мЈјлЎң YAML лҳҗлҠ” JSON нҳ•мӢқмқҳ м„Өм • нҢҢмқјмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•  л•Ң мӮ¬мҡ©лҗңлӢӨ. kubectl create -f ${POD_NAME}.yaml YAML лҳҗлҠ” JSON нҢҢмқјмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙ лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•ңлӢӨ. лӢӨм–‘н•ң мў…лҘҳмқҳ Kubernetes лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.(мҳҲ, Pod, Service, Deployment, ConfigMap л“ұ) лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј м •мқҳн•ң нҢҢмқјмқ„ лӘ…нҷ•н•ҳкІҢ мҡ”кө¬н•ңлӢӨ. kubectl run kubectl run лӘ…л №м–ҙлҠ” мЈјлЎң лӢЁмҲңн•ң н…ҢмҠӨнҠё лӘ©м ҒмқҙлӮҳ мһ„мӢңлЎң PodлҘј мғқм„ұн•  л•Ң мӮ¬мҡ©лҗңлӢӨ. мқҙ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҠ” PodлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳкі , к·ё PodлҘј кё°л°ҳмңјлЎң лӢЁмҲңнһҲ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқ„ мӢӨн–үн•ҳлҠ” лҚ° мӮ¬мҡ©лҗңлӢӨ. kubectl run лӘ…л №м–ҙлҠ” кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң Deployment лҰ¬мҶҢлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳм§Җл§Ң, нҠ№м • мҳөм…ҳмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙ лӢЁмҲңн•ң PodлҸ„ мғқм„ұн•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. kubectl run nginx --image=nginx лӘ…л №м–ҙ н•ң мӨ„лЎң мүҪкІҢ Pod лҳҗлҠ” DeploymentлҘј мғқм„ұн•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң Deployment лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳл©°, н•„мҡ”м—җ л”°лқј лӢӨлҘё лҰ¬мҶҢмҠӨ мў…лҘҳлҘј м§Җм •н•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. м„Өм • нҢҢмқј м—Ҷмқҙ лӘ…л №м–ҙ мқёмһҗлЎң н•„мҡ”н•ң мҳөм…ҳмқ„ м§Җм •н•ңлӢӨ. мһ„мӢң PodлӮҳ к°„лӢЁн•ң м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳ л°°нҸ¬м—җ мң мҡ©н•ҳлӢӨ.
title: Kubernetes Core Concepts

content: 2. File save - PythonOperator
title: Cryptocurrency Data Pipeline

content: DAG from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator def _get_crypto_data(): session = Session() session.headers.update(headers) try: for crypto in CRYPTO_LIST: response = session.get(URL + crypto) response.raise_for_status() data = response.json() filename = f"{crypto}_data.json" filepath = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename) with open(filepath, "w") as f: json.dump(data, f, indent=2) print(f"Data for {crypto} saved to {filepath}") except (ConnectionError, Timeout, TooManyRedirects) as e: print(e) except Exception as e: print(f"Unexpected error: {e}") get_crypto_data = PythonOperator( task_id="get_crypto_data", python_callable=_get_crypto_data, )

content: 3. Files upload to HDFS - BashOperator, PythonOperator
title: Cryptocurrency Data Pipeline

content: DAG from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator def _generate_hdfs_commands(): files = os.listdir(OUTPUT_DIR) # print(files) commands = [] for filename in files: if filename.endswith(".json"): commands.append( f"hdfs dfs -put -f {os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)} /crypto" ) return commands def _save_files_to_hdfs(): commands = _generate_hdfs_commands() for command in commands: os.system(command) print(f"Executed: {command}") make_directory_to_hdfs = BashOperator( task_id="make_directory_to_hdfs", bash_command="hdfs dfs -mkdir -p /crypto", ) save_files_to_hdfs = PythonOperator( task_id="save_files_to_hdfs", python_callable=_save_files_to_hdfs, ) make_directory_to_hdfs >> save_files_to_hdfs

content: 3. Download data task - PythonOperator
title: Forex Data Pipeline

content: DAG from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator import csv import requests import json def download_rates(): BASE_URL = "https://gist.githubusercontent.com/marclamberti/f45f872dea4dfd3eaa015a4a1af4b39b/raw/" ENDPOINTS = { "USD": "api_forex_exchange_usd.json", "EUR": "api_forex_exchange_eur.json", } with open("/opt/airflow/dags/files/forex_currencies.csv") as forex_currencies: reader = csv.DictReader(forex_currencies, delimiter=";") for idx, row in enumerate(reader): base = row["base"] with_pairs = row["with_pairs"].split(" ") indata = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}{ENDPOINTS[base]}").json() outdata = {"base": base, "rates": {}, "last_update": indata["date"]} for pair in with_pairs: outdata["rates"][pair] = indata["rates"][pair] with open("/opt/airflow/dags/files/forex_rates.json", "a") as outfile: json.dump(outdata, outfile) outfile.write("\n") with DAG( "forex_data_pipeline", start_date=datetime(2024, 1, 1), schedule_interval="@daily", default_args=default_args, catchup=False, ) as dag: ... # Download the forex rates from the API - PythonOperator downloading_rates = PythonOperator( task_id="downloading_rates", python_callable=download_rates, )

content: Docker нҢҢмқј # Base Image FROM python:3.8-slim LABEL maintainer="seilylook" # Arguments that can be set with docker build ARG AIRFLOW_VERSION=2.0.2 ARG AIRFLOW_HOME=/opt/airflow # Export the environment variable AIRFLOW_HOME where airflow will be installed ENV AIRFLOW_HOME=${AIRFLOW_HOME} # Install dependencies and tools RUN apt-get update -yqq && \ apt-get upgrade -yqq && \ apt-get install -yqq --no-install-recommends \ wget \ libczmq-dev \ curl \ libssl-dev \ git \ inetutils-telnet \ bind9utils freetds-dev \ libkrb5-dev \ libsasl2-dev \ libffi-dev libpq-dev \ freetds-bin build-essential \ default-libmysqlclient-dev \ apt-utils \ rsync \ zip \ unzip \ gcc \ vim \ locales \ && apt-get clean COPY ./constraints-3.8.txt /constraints-3.8.txt # Upgrade pip # Create airflow user # Install apache airflow with subpackages RUN pip install --upgrade pip && \ useradd -ms /bin/bash -d ${AIRFLOW_HOME} airflow && \ pip install apache-airflow[postgres]==${AIRFLOW_VERSION} --constraint /constraints-3.8.txt # Copy the entrypoint.sh from host to container (at path AIRFLOW_HOME) COPY ./entrypoint.sh ./entrypoint.sh # Set the entrypoint.sh file to be executable RUN chmod +x ./entrypoint.sh # Set the owner of the files in AIRFLOW_HOME to the user airflow RUN chown -R airflow: ${AIRFLOW_HOME} # Set the username to use USER airflow # Set workdir (it's like a cd inside the container) WORKDIR ${AIRFLOW_HOME} # Create the dags folder which will contain the DAGs RUN mkdir dags # Expose ports (just to indicate that this container needs to map port) EXPOSE 8080 # Execute the entrypoint.sh ENTRYPOINT [ "/entrypoint.sh" ]

content: Configuration
title: Snowflake

content: SnowSQLм„Өм№ҳ
title: Snowflake

content: SnowSQLм—°кІ°
title: Snowflake

content: Airflow is a platform that lets you build and run workflows. A workflow is represented as a **DAG** (a Directed Acyclic Graph), and contains individual pieces of work called Tasks, arranged with dependencies and data flows taken into account. A DAG specifies the dependencies between tasks, which defines the order in which to execute the tasks. Tasks describe what to do, be it fetching data, running analysis, triggering other systems, or more. Airflow itself is agnostic to what youвҖҷre running - it will happily orchestrate and run anything, either with high-level support from one of our providers, or directly as a command using the shell or Python Operators. Airflow components
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Required components
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Scheduler handles both triggering scheduled workflows, and submitting Tasks to the executor to run. The executor, is a configuration property of the scheduler, not a separate component and runs within the scheduler process. There are several executors available out of the box, and you can also write your own.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Webserver presents a handy user interface to inspect, trigger and debug the behaviour of DAGs and tasks.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Folder of DAGs read by the scheduler to figure out what tasks to run and when and to run them.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Metadata database airflow components use to store state of workflows and tasks. Setting up a metadata database is described in Set up a Database Backend and is required for Airflow to work.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Optional components Optional worker, which executes the tasks given to it by the scheduler. In the basic installation worker might be part of the scheduler not a separate component. It can be run as a long running process in the CeleryExecutor, or as a POD in the KubernetesExecutor. Optional **triggerer**, which executes deferred tasks in an asyncio event loop. In basic installation where deferred tasks are not used, a triggerer is not necessary. Optional **DAG processor**, which parses DAG files and serializes them into the metadata database. By default, the dag processor process is part of the scheduler, but it can be run as a separate component for scalability and security reasons. If dag processor is present scheduler does not need to read the **DAG** files directly. Optional folder of plugins. Plugins are a way to extend AirflowвҖҷs functionality (similar to installed packages). Plugins are read by the scheduler, dag processor, triggerer and webserver. Deploying Airflow components All the components are Python applications that can be deployed using various deployment mechanisms. They can have extra installed packages installed in their Python environment. This is useful for example to install custom operators or sensors or extend Airflow functionality with custom plugins. While Airflow can be run in a single machine and with simple installation where only scheduler and webserver are deployed, Airflow is designed to be scalable and secure, and is able to run in a distributed environment - where various components can run on different machines, with different security perimeters and can be scaled by running multiple instances of the components above. The separation of components also allow for increased security, by isolating the components from each other and by allowing to perform different tasks. For example separating dag processor from scheduler allows to make sure that the scheduler does not have access to the DAG files and cannot execute code provided by DAG author. brown solid lines represent DAG files submission and synchronization blue solid lines represent deploying and accessing installed packages and plugins black dashed(м җм„ ) lines represent control flow of workers by the scheduler (via executor) black solid lines represent accessing the UI to manage execution of the workflows red dashed(м җм„ ) lines represent accessing the metadata database by all components
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Basic Airflow deployment This is the simplest deployment of Airflow, usually operated and managed on a single machine. Such a deployment usually uses the LocalExecutor, where the scheduler and the workers are in the same Python process and the DAG files are read directly from the local filesystem by the scheduler. The webserver runs on the same machine as the scheduler. There is no triggerer component, which means that task deferral is not possible. Such an installation typically does not separate user roles - deployment, configuration, operation, authoring and maintenance are all done by the same person and there are no security perimeters between the components.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Distributed Airflow architecture This is the architecture of Airflow where components of Airflow are distributed among multiple machines and where various roles of users are introduced - Deployment Manager, DAG author, Operations User. In the case of a distributed deployment, it is important to consider the security aspects of the components. The webserver does not have access to the DAG files directly. The code in the Code tab of the UI is read from the metadata database. The webserver cannot execute any code submitted by the DAG author. It can only execute code that is installed as an installed package or plugin by the Deployment Manager. The Operations User only has access to the UI and can only trigger DAGs and tasks, but cannot author DAGs. The DAG files need to be synchronized between all the components that use them - scheduler, triggerer and workers. The DAG files can be synchronized by various mechanisms - typical ways how DAGs can be synchronized are described in Manage DAGs files ot our Helm Chart documentation. Helm chart is one of the ways how to deploy Airflow in Kubernetes cluster.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: Seperate DAG processing architecture In a more complex installation where security and isolation are important, youвҖҷll also see the standalone dag processor component that allows to separate scheduler from accessing DAG files. This is suitable if the deployment focus is on isolation between parsed tasks. While Airflow does not yet support full multi-tenant features, it can be used to make sure that DAG author provided code is never executed in the context of the scheduler. Workloads A DAG runs through a series of Tasks, and there are three common types of task you will see: Operators, predefined tasks that you can string together quickly to build most parts of your DAGs. Sensors, a special subclass of Operators which are entirely about waiting for an external event to happen. A TaskFlow-decorated @task, which is a custom Python function packaged up as a Task. Internally, these are all actually subclasses of AirflowвҖҷs BaseOperator, and the concepts of Task and Operator are somewhat interchangeable, but itвҖҷs useful to think of them as separate concepts - essentially, Operators and Sensors are templates, and when you call one in a DAG file, youвҖҷre making a Task.
title: Airflow Architecture

content: RDD Resilient Distributed Datasets(RDDs) distrubuted collections of objects that can be cached in memory across cluster manipulated through pararrel operators automatically recomputed on failure immutable(read-only)
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: RDD м—°мӮ° RDD м—°мӮ°мқҖ нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳкіј м•Ўм…ҳмқҙ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳмқҖ RDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҙм„ң мғҲлЎңмҡҙ RDDлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳкі , м•Ўм…ҳмқҖ RDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҙм„ң мһ‘м—…мқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•ҳм—¬ кІ°кіјлҘј л“ңлқјмқҙлІ„м—җ л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, нҢҢмқјмӢңмҠӨн…ңм—җ кІ°кіјлҘј м“°лҠ” м—°мӮ°мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬лҠ” нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳмқ„ нҳём¶ңн•  л•ҢлҠ” мһ‘м—…мқ„ кө¬м„ұн•ҳкі , м•Ўм…ҳмқҙ нҳём¶ң лҗ  л•Ң мӢӨм ң кі„мӮ°мқ„ мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. лӢӨмқҢмқҳ мҳҲм ңм—җм„ң csvнҢҢмқјмқҳ лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј мқҪм–ҙм„ң linesлқјлҠ” RDD к°қмІҙлҘј мғқм„ұн•ҳкі , к°Ғ лқјмқёмқҳ кёҖмһҗмқҳ к°ңмҲҳлҘј м„ёлҠ” map нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳ н•ЁмҲҳлҘј нҳём¶ңн•ҳкі , кёҖмһҗ мҲҳмқҳ мҙқн•©мқ„ кө¬н•ҳлҠ” reduce м•Ўм…ҳ н•ЁмҲҳлҘј нҳём¶ңн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. map н•ЁмҲҳлҘј нҳём¶ңн•  л•ҢлҠ” мһ‘м—…мқҙ м§„н–үлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡкі , reduce н•ЁмҲҳлҘј нҳём¶ңн•  л•Ң нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„°м—җм„ң мһ‘м—…мқҙ м§„н–үлҗҳлҠ” кІғмқ„ нҷ•мқё н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Transformations нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳмқҖ RDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј ліҖнҷҳн•ҳкі  RDDлҘј л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳлҠ” мһ‘м—…мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. мЈјмҡ”н•ЁмҲҳлҠ” лӢӨмқҢкіј к°ҷмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. н•ЁмҲҳ м„ӨлӘ… map(func) funcлЎң мІҳлҰ¬лҗң мғҲлЎңмҡҙ лҚ°мқҙн„° м…Ӣ ліҖнҷҳ filter(func) funcм—җм„ң trueлҘј л°ҳнҷҳн•ң к°’мңјлЎң н•„н„°л§Ғ flatMap(func) funcлҠ” л°°м—ҙ(нҳ№мқҖ Seq)мқ„ л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳкі , мқҙ л°°м—ҙл“Өмқ„ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ л°°м—ҙлЎң л°ҳнҷҳ distinct([numPartitions]) лҚ°мқҙн„°м…Ӣмқҳ мӨ‘ліөмқ„ м ңкұ° groupByKey([numPartitions]) нӮӨлҘј кё°мӨҖмңјлЎң к·ёлЈ№н•‘ мІҳлҰ¬. (K, V) мҢҚмқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•ҳм—¬ (K, Iterable)лЎң л°ҳнҷҳ reduceByKey(func, [numPartitions]) нӮӨлҘј кё°мӨҖмңјлЎң мЈјм–ҙм§„ funcлЎң мІҳлҰ¬лҗң мһ‘м—… кІ°кіјлҘј (K, V)лЎң л°ҳнҷҳ sortByKey([ascending], [numPartitions]) нӮӨлҘј кё°мӨҖмңјлЎң м •л ¬ нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳмқҖ лӢӨмқҢмІҳлҹј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. cctvRDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мІҳлҰ¬н•ң нҠёлһңмҠӨнҸ¬л©”мқҙм…ҳмқҖ кІ°кіјк°’мңјлЎң RDDлҘј л°ҳнҷҳн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. take м•Ўм…ҳмқҙ нҳём¶ңлҗҳкё° м „м—җлҠ” мӢӨм ң мһ‘м—…мқ„ м§„н–үн•ҳм§Җ м•ҠмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Actions м•Ўм…ҳмқҖ RDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мһ‘м—…мқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•ң кІ°кіјлҘј л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳлҠ” мһ‘м—…мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. мЈјмҡ” н•ЁмҲҳлҠ” лӢӨмқҢкіј к°ҷмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. н•ЁмҲҳ м„ӨлӘ… reduce(func) funcлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҙ лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј м§‘кі„(л‘җ к°ңмқҳ мқёмҲҳлҘј л°ӣм•„м„ң н•ҳлӮҳлҘј л°ҳнҷҳ). лі‘л ¬ мІҳлҰ¬к°Җ к°ҖлҠҘн•ҙм•ј н•Ё collect() мІҳлҰ¬ кІ°кіјлҘј л°°м—ҙлЎң л°ҳнҷҳ. н•„н„°л§Ғ л“ұ мһ‘мқҖ лҚ°мқҙн„° м§‘н•©мқ„ л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳлҠ”лҚ° мң мҡ© count() лҚ°мқҙн„°мқҳ к°ңмҲҳ л°ҳнҷҳ first() лҚ°мқҙн„°м…Ӣмқҳ мІ«лІҲм§ё м•„мқҙн…ң л°ҳнҷҳ(take(1)кіј мң мӮ¬) take(n) лҚ°мқҙн„°м…Ӣмқҳ мІ«лІҲм§ёл¶Җн„° nк°ңмқҳ л°°м—ҙмқ„ л°ҳнҷҳ saveAsTextFile(path) лҚ°мқҙн„°м…Ӣмқ„ н…ҚмҠӨнҠё нҢҢмқјлЎң м§Җм •н•ң мң„м№ҳм—җ м ҖмһҘ countByKey() нӮӨлҘј кё°мӨҖмңјлЎң м№ҙмҡҙнҠё л°ҳнҷҳ foreach(func) лҚ°мқҙн„°м…Ӣмқҳ к°Ғ м—ҳлҰ¬лЁјнҠёлҘј funcлЎң мІҳлҰ¬. ліҙнҶө AccumulatorмҷҖ н•Ёк»ҳ мӮ¬мҡ© м•Ўм…ҳмқҖ лӢӨмқҢмІҳлҹј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. cctvRDDлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мІҳлҰ¬н•ң м•Ўм…ҳмқҖ кІ°кіјлҘј л“ңлқјмқҙлІ„(мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬мүҳ)м—җ л°ҳнҷҳн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, нҢҢмқјлЎң м ҖмһҘн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: н•ЁмҲҳ м „лӢ¬ RDD м—°мӮ°мқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•  л•Ң л§ӨлІҲ мһ‘м—…мқ„ кө¬нҳ„н•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡкі , н•ЁмҲҳлЎң кө¬нҳ„н•ҳм—¬ мһ‘м—…мқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•  мҲҳлҸ„ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. н•ЁмҲҳлҘј м „лӢ¬ н•  л•ҢлҠ” мҷёл¶Җмқҳ ліҖмҲҳлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ” мҲңмҲҳ н•ЁмҲҳлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳлҠ” кІғмқҙ мўӢмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„° нҷҳкІҪм—җм„ң мҷёл¶Җ ліҖмҲҳмқҳ мӮ¬мҡ©мқҖ мһҳ лӘ»лҗң кІ°кіјлҘј мғқм„ұн•  к°ҖлҠҘм„ұмқҙ лҶ’кё° л•Ңл¬ёмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: мәҗмү¬ мқҙмҡ© RDDлҠ” мІҳлҰ¬ кІ°кіјлҘј л©”лӘЁлҰ¬лӮҳ л””мҠӨнҒ¬м—җ м ҖмһҘн•ҳкі  лӢӨмқҢ кі„мӮ°м—җ мқҙмҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. л°ҳліөмһ‘м—…мқҳ кІҪмҡ° мқҙ мәҗмү¬лҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҙм„ң мІҳлҰ¬ мҶҚлҸ„лҘј лҶ’мқј мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң лӢЁмқјмһ‘м—…мқҳ кІҪмҡ° лҚ°мқҙн„° ліөмӮ¬лҘј мң„н•ң мҳӨлІ„н—Өл“ңк°Җ л°ңмғқн•ҳм—¬ мІҳлҰ¬мӢңк°„мқҙ лҚ” лҠҗл Өм§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. л”°лқјм„ң мһ‘м—…мқҳ мў…лҘҳмҷҖ мҳҒн–Ҙмқ„ нҢҢм•…н•ң нӣ„м—җ мәҗмҠҒмқ„ мқҙмҡ©н•ҳлҠ” кІғмқҙ мўӢмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. RDDлҠ” persist(), cache() л©”мҶҢл“ңлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мәҗмҠҒмқ„ м§Җмӣҗн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. мәҗмҠҒн•ң лҚ°мқҙн„°м—җ л¬ём ңк°Җ мғқкё°л©ҙ мһҗлҸҷмңјлЎң ліөкө¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ. лҳҗн•ң м ҖмһҘ л°©лІ•мқ„ м„Өм •н•  мҲҳ мһҲм–ҙм„ң, л©”лӘЁлҰ¬лӮҳ л””мҠӨнҒ¬м—җ м ҖмһҘ н•  мҲҳлҸ„ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. м„Өм • м„ӨлӘ… MEMORY_ONLY RDDлҘј л©”лӘЁлҰ¬мғҒм—җ м ҖмһҘ. л©”лӘЁлҰ¬ліҙлӢӨ мҡ©лҹүмқҙ нҒ¬л©ҙ мқјл¶Җл§Ң м ҖмһҘн•ҳкі  н•„мҡ”н•  л•Ңл§ҲлӢӨ кі„мӮ°. кё°ліёк°’ MEMORY_AND_DISK RDDлҘј л©”лӘЁлҰ¬мғҒм—җ м ҖмһҘ. л©”лӘЁлҰ¬ліҙлӢӨ мҡ©лҹүмқҙ нҒ¬л©ҙ мқјл¶ҖлҠ” л©”лӘЁлҰ¬, мқјл¶ҖлҠ” л””мҠӨнҒ¬м—җ м ҖмһҘ DISK_ONLY RDDлҘј л””мҠӨнҒ¬м—җ м ҖмһҘ
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Key, ValueлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ң мІҳлҰ¬ мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬лҠ” MapReduceмІҳлҹј (key, value)мҢҚмқ„ мқҙмҡ©н•ң мІҳлҰ¬лҸ„ к°ҖлҠҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң м ңкіөн•ҳлҠ” flatMap, reduceByKey, groupByKey, mapValues, sortByKeyлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҙм„ң мўҖ лҚ” нҺёлҰ¬н•ң мІҳлҰ¬к°Җ к°ҖлҠҘн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. лӢӨмқҢмқҳ мӣҢл“ң м№ҙмҡҙнҠёлҠ” нӮӨ, л°ёлҘҳлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ң мІҳлҰ¬лҘј нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. нҢҢмқјмқҳ лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј мқҪм–ҙм„ң flatMapлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ лӢЁм–ҙлі„лЎң л¶„лҰ¬н•ҳкі , mapмқ„ мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ лӢЁм–ҙмқҳ к°ңмҲҳлҘј м„ём–ҙмӨҚлӢҲлӢӨ. reduceByKeyлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ лӢЁм–ҙлі„лЎң к·ёлЈ№нҷ” н•ҳм—¬ лӢЁм–ҙк°Җ лӮҳнғҖлӮң к°ңмҲҳлҘј м„ём–ҙмӨҚлӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Accumulator мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬лҠ” PCм—җм„ң лӢЁлҸ…мңјлЎң мІҳлҰ¬лҗҳлҠ” кІғмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„°м—җм„ң мІҳлҰ¬н•ҳкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ нҒҙлЎңм ё1лҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ кІ°кіјк°Җ лӢ¬лқјм§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. лӢӨмқҢмқҳ мҳҲм ңмҷҖ к°ҷмқҙ foreach() л°ҳліөл¬ём—җ мҷёл¶Җм—җ м„ м–ёлҗң sumValue ліҖмҲҳм—җ лӘЁл“  к°’мқ„ лҚ”н•ҳлҠ” н•ЁмҲҳлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ мӢӨн–ү лӘЁл“ң(local vs cluster)м—җ л”°лқј кІ°кіјк°Җ лӢ¬лқј м§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. лЎңм»¬ лӘЁл“ңм—җм„ңлҠ” мӣҗн•ҳлҠ” кІ°кіјк°Җ лӮҳмҳӨм§Җл§Ң, нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„° лӘЁл“ңм—җм„ңлҠ” к°Ғ л…ёл“ңм—җм„ң лЎңм»¬мқҳ sumValue ліҖмҲҳмқҳ к°’мқ„ мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мһ‘м—…мқ„ мІҳлҰ¬н•ҳкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ кІ°кіјк°Җ лӢ¬лқјм§‘лӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Broadcast лёҢлЎңл“ң мәҗмҠӨнҠёлҠ” л§өлҰ¬л“ҖмҠӨмқҳ л””мҠӨнҠёлҰ¬л·°нҠё мәҗмү¬(distribute cache)мҷҖ мң мӮ¬н•ң м—ӯнҷңмқ„ н•ҳлҠ” лӘЁл“  л…ёл“ңм—җм„ң кіөмң лҗҳлҠ” мқҪкё° м „мҡ© к°’мһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. broadcast() мқҙмҡ©н•ҳм—¬ мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. мЎ°мқём—җ мқҙмҡ©лҗҳлҠ” к°’л“Өмқ„ м„ м–ён•ҳм—¬ мқҙмҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. лӢӨмқҢмқҳ мҳҲм ңм—җм„ң broadcastVar ліҖмҲҳлҠ” нҒҙлҹ¬мҠӨн„°мқҳ лӘЁл“  л…ёл“ңм—җм„ң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” к°’мқҙ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Shuffle мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬м—җм„ң мЎ°мқё, м •л ¬ мһ‘м—…мқҖ м…”н”Ң(Shuffle) мһ‘м—…мқ„ мӢӨн–үн•©лӢҲлӢӨ. м…”н”ҢмқҖ нҢҢнӢ°м…ҳк°„м—җ к·ёлЈ№нҷ”лҗң лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј л°°нҸ¬н•ҳлҠ” л©”м»ӨлӢҲмҰҳмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ. м…”н”ҢмқҖ мһ„мӢң нҢҢмқјмқҳ ліөмӮ¬, мқҙлҸҷмқҙ мһҲкё° лҢҖл¬ём—җ л§ҺмқҖ л№„мҡ©мқҙ л“ӨкІҢ лҗ©лӢҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Lazy Evaluation Lazy Evaluation means when calling a transformation on an RDD(map), the operation is not immmediately performed. Spark internally records metadata to indicate that this operation is requested. It is best to think of each RDD as consisting of instructions on how to compute the data of transformations.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: м •лҰ¬ Transformations mean setting functions. Actions mean performing the functions.
title: Spark RDD(Resilient Distributed Dataset)

content: Introduction Spark Cache and Persist are optimization techniques for iterative and interactive Spark applications to improve the performances of the jobs or applications. In Key Point RDD.cache() cashes the RDD with the default storage level MEMORY_ONLY DataFrame.cache() caches the DataFrame with the default storage level MEMORY_AND_DISK The persist() method is used to store it to the user-defined storage level On Spark UI, the Storage tab shows where partitions exist in memory or disk across the cluster. DataFrame cache() is an alias for persist(StorageLevel.MEMORY_AND_DISK). Caching of Spark DataFrame or DataSet is a lazy operation, meaing a DataFrame will not be cached until you trigger an action.
title: Spark Cache Persist

content: Spark Cache VS Persist Usring cache() and persist() methods, Spark provides an optimization mechanism to store the intermediate computation of an RDD, DataFrame, and Dataset so they can be reused in subsequent actions(reusing the RDD, Dataframe, and Dataset computation results). Both caching and persisting are used to save the Spark RDD, Dataframe, and Datasets. But, the difference is, RDD cache() method default saves it to memory (MEMORY_ONLY) and, DataFrame cache() method default saves it to memory (MEMORY_AND_DISK), whereas persist() method is used to store it to the user-defined storage level. When you persist a dataset, each node stores its partitioned data in memory and reuses them in other actions on that dataset. And SparkвҖҷs persisted data on nodes are fault-tolerant meaning if any partition of a Dataset is lost, it will automatically be recomputed using the original transformations that created it.
title: Spark Cache Persist

content: Persist() storage levels All different storage level Spark supports are available at org.apache.spark.storage.StorageLevel class. The storage level specifies how and where to persist or cache a Spark DataFrame and Dataset. MEMORY_ONLY вҖ“ This is the default behavior of the RDD cache() method and stores the RDD or DataFrame as deserialized objects to JVM memory. When there is no enough memory available it will not save DataFrame of some partitions and these will be re-computed as and when required. This takes more memory. but unlike RDD, this would be slower than MEMORY_AND_DISK level as it recomputes the unsaved partitions and recomputing the in-memory columnar representation of the underlying table is expensive MEMORY_ONLY_SER вҖ“ This is the same as MEMORY_ONLY but the difference being it stores RDD as serialized objects to JVM memory. It takes lesser memory (space-efficient) then MEMORY_ONLY as it saves objects as serialized and takes an additional few more CPU cycles in order to deserialize. MEMORY_ONLY_2 вҖ“ Same as MEMORY_ONLY storage level but replicate each partition to two cluster nodes. MEMORY_ONLY_SER_2 вҖ“ Same as MEMORY_ONLY_SER storage level but replicate each partition to two cluster nodes. MEMORY_AND_DISK вҖ“ This is the default behavior of the DataFrame or Dataset. In this Storage Level, The DataFrame will be stored in JVM memory as a deserialized object. When required storage is greater than available memory, it stores some of the excess partitions into the disk and reads the data from the disk when required. It is slower as there is I/O involved. MEMORY_AND_DISK_SER вҖ“ This is the same as MEMORY_AND_DISK storage level difference being it serializes the DataFrame objects in memory and on disk when space is not available. MEMORY_AND_DISK_2 вҖ“ Same as MEMORY_AND_DISK storage level but replicate each partition to two cluster nodes. MEMORY_AND_DISK_SER_2 вҖ“ Same as MEMORY_AND_DISK_SER storage level but replicate each partition to two cluster nodes. DISK_ONLY вҖ“ In this storage level, DataFrame is stored only on disk and the CPU computation time is high as I/O is involved. DISK_ONLY_2 вҖ“ Same as DISK_ONLY storage level but replicate each partition to two cluster nodes.
title: Spark Cache Persist

content: Conclusion Spark automatically monitors every persist() and cache() calls you make and it checks usage on each node and drops persisted data if not used or using least-recently-used (LRU) algorithm. You can also manually remove using unpersist() method. Spark caching and persistance is just one of the optimization techniques to improve the performance of Spark jobs.
title: Spark Cache Persist

content: Introduction Jobs: мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҳ лӘЁл“  jobм—җ лҢҖн•ң мҡ”м•Ҫ м •ліҙ Stages: лӘЁл“  jobsмқҳ лӘЁл“  stagesмқҳ нҳ„мһ¬ мғҒнғң мҡ”м•Ҫ м •ліҙ Storage: persisted RDDмҷҖ DataFrame м •ліҙ м ңкіө Environment: лӢӨм–‘н•ң нҷҳкІҪ ліҖмҲҳ к°’ Executors: м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқ„ мң„н•ҙ мғқм„ұлҗң Executer м •ліҙ м ңкіө. л©”лӘЁлҰ¬мҷҖ л””мҠӨнҒ¬ мӮ¬мҡ©лҹүкіј task, shuffle м •ліҙ л“ұ SQL: м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҙ Spark SQL мҝјлҰ¬ мӢӨн–ү мӢң м •ліҙ м ңкіө Streaming: Streaming jobs мӢӨн–ү мӢң м •ліҙ м ңкіө

content: Job нғӯ мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬мқҳ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҳ actionмқҖ н•ҳлӮҳ мқҙмғҒмқҳ Jobмқ„ мғқм„ұн•ҳкі  мӢӨн–үн•ңлӢӨ. Job нғӯмқҳ лһңл”© нҺҳмқҙм§Җм—җм„ңлҠ” к·ёлҹ¬н•ң Jobл“Өмқҳ 1) кё°ліё м •ліҙ 2) мқҙлІӨнҠё нғҖмһ„лқјмқё 3)мӢӨн–үлҗҳкұ°лӮҳ мҷ„лЈҢлҗң Job м •ліҙлҘј ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ. кё°ліё м •ліҙ User: нҳ„мһ¬ мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳ мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ Total Uptime: мҠӨнҢҢнҒ¬ м• н”ҢлҰ¬мјҖмқҙм…ҳмқҙ мІ« мӢңмһ‘ мқҙнӣ„мқҳ мӢңк°„ Completed Jobs: мҷ„лЈҢлҗң Jobsмқҳ к°ңмҲҳ мқҙлІӨнҠё нғҖмһ„лқјмқё: мӢңк°„ мҲңмңјлЎң ExecutorsмҷҖ Jobsмқҳ мғҒнғңлҘј к·ёлһҳн”ҪмңјлЎң ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ. 2)мқҳ мғҒлӢЁм—җлҠ” н•ң к°ңмқҳ мғҒмһҗк°Җ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Executor, н•ҳлӢЁм—җм„ңлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ мғҒмһҗк°Җ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ Jobм—җ н•ҙлӢ№лҗҳлҠ” кІғмқ„ ліј мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мғҳн”Ң мҪ”л“ңлҘј мӢӨн–үн•ҳл©ҙ 2)мқҳ мҳӨлҘёмӘҪ н•ҳлӢЁмқҳ 11:15 мқҙнӣ„мқҳ 3к°ңмқҳ Jobмқҙ мғқм„ұлҗң кІғмқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. Completed Jobs: мҷ„лЈҢлҗң Jobл“Өмқ„ Description, м ңм¶ң мӢңк°„, мҶҢмҡ© мӢңк°„, Stage к°ңмҲҳ, Task к°ңмҲҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҙ лӮҳм—ҙн•ҙмӨҖлӢӨ. мӢӨн–үлҗҳкі  мһҲлҠ” Jobмқҙ мһҲлӢӨл©ҙ мқҙ л¶Җл¶„ мң„м—җ Active Jobsм—җм„ң лҸҷмқјн•ң нҳ•нғңлЎң ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ. 3)мқҳ нҢҢлһ‘мғү л§ҒнҒ¬лҘј нҒҙлҰӯн•ҙ к°Ғ Jobмқҳ Detail нҺҳмқҙм§ҖлЎң мқҙлҸҷн•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark UI

content: Job Detail нғӯ Job кё°ліё м •ліҙм—җм„ңлҠ” м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ мӮ¬н•ӯмқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. Status: Jobмқҳ мғҒнғң Submitted: Job м ңм¶ң мқјмӢң Duration: Job мҲҳн–ү мӢңк°„ Associated SQL Query: м—°кҙҖ SQL Query лІҲнҳё Job Group: н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ м•Ўм…ҳмқҖ н•ҳлӮҳ мқҙмғҒмқҳ Jobмқ„ мғқм„ұн•ңлӢӨ. Completed Stages: мҷ„лЈҢ мҠӨн…Ңмқҙм§Җ мҲҳ Skipped Stages: мғқлһөлҗң мҠӨн…Ңмқҙм§Җ мҲҳ. мәҗмӢңм—җ лҚ°мқҙн„°лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” л“ұмқҳ мқҙмң лЎң мһ¬мӢӨн–үмқҙ н•„мҡ” м—Ҷм–ҙ мғқлһөлҗң кІҪмҡ°. мқҙлІӨнҠё нғҖмһ„лқјмқё м—җм„ңлҠ” мқҙлІҲм—җлҠ” Stage лі„лЎң н‘ңмӢңлҗҳкІҢ лҗңлӢӨ. DAG мӢңк°Ғнҷ”: Jobм—җ нҸ¬н•Ёлҗң Stagesл“Өмқҳ DAG(Directed Acyclic Graph)лҘј мӢңк°Ғм ҒмңјлЎң нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мң„м—җм„ңлҠ” 16, 17 Stageк°Җ Skipped лҗҳкі , 18 Stageл§Ң мӢӨн–үлҗң кІғмқ„ ліј мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. к°Ғ Stageмқҳ мҡ”мҶҢлҘј мӮҙнҺҙліҙл©° лҢҖлһөм Ғмқё мһ‘м—…нқҗлҰ„мқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мҷ„лЈҢ л°Ҹ мғқлһө Stages: Stageлі„лЎң м¶•м•Ҫлҗң м •ліҙмҷҖ н•Ёк»ҳ мЈјмҡ” м •ліҙл“Өмқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мӢӨн–үлҗҳлҠ” Pool мқҙлҰ„, StageлЎң мқҙлҸҷн•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” л§ҒнҒ¬к°Җ мһҲлҠ” Description, м ңм¶ң мқјмӢң, мҶҢмҡ” мӢңк°„, Tasks, мһ…м¶ңл Ҙ мӮ¬мқҙмҰҲмҷҖ Shuffle Read & Write мӮ¬мқҙмҰҲк°Җ к·ёлҹ¬н•ң м •ліҙм—җ нҸ¬н•ЁлҗңлӢӨ. Task л ҲлІЁлЎң scale down н•ҳкё°м „м—җ Stage л ҲлІЁм—җм„ң нҠ№м • мһ‘м—…мқҙ л„Ҳл¬ҙ л§ҺмқҖ TaskлҘј л°ңмғқмӢңнӮӨм§ҖлҠ” м•ҠлҠ”м§Җ, Shuffle I/Oк°Җ кіјлҸ„н•ҳм§ҖлҠ” м•ҠмқҖм§Җ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.
title: Spark UI

content: Stages нғӯ Stages нғӯмқҳ нҺҳмқҙм§Җм—җм„ңлҠ” Jobs нғӯкіј мң мӮ¬н•ҳкІҢ мҷ„лЈҢ л°Ҹ мӢӨн–ү мӨ‘мқё м—¬лҹ¬ Stagesм—җ лҢҖн•ң м •ліҙлҘј нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. лҳҗн•ң, к°ңлі„ Stage нҺҳмқҙм§ҖлЎң мқҙлҸҷн•ҙ м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҙ мғҒм„ё м •ліҙлҘј нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. нҠ№м§•м ҒмңјлЎң мӨ‘мҡ”н•ң л¶Җл¶„мқҖ м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҙ лі‘л ¬мқҳ мөңмҶҢ лӢЁмң„мқё Taskл“Өмқҳ мӢӨн–ү мқҙлІӨнҠё нғҖмһ„лқјмқёкіј лӢӨм–‘н•ң к°ңлі„ лҳҗлҠ” м§‘кі„ л©”нҠёлҰӯмқ„ нҷ•мқён•  мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” л¶Җл¶„мқҙлӢӨ. Job, Stage л ҲлІЁм—җм„ң нҠ№м • нҳ„мғҒм—җ лҢҖн•ң м „мІҙм Ғмқё нқҗлҰ„мқ„ лҙӨлӢӨл©ҙ, м•„лһҳмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ к°ңлі„ TaskлҘј нғҗмғүн•ҳл©ҙм„ң мқјл¶Җ мқҙмғҒм№ҳ лҳҗлҠ” нҳ„мғҒм—җ лҢҖн•ҙ лҚ”мҡұ м„ёл°Җн•ң л¶„м„қмқ„ м§„н–үн•  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мң„мҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ л©”нҠёлҰӯм—җм„ңлҠ” н•ҙлӢ№ Stageм—җ мҶҚн•ҳлҠ” 200к°ңмқҳ Taskл“Өм—җ лҢҖн•ң лӢӨм–‘н•ң мҲҳм№ҳлҘј мҡ”м•Ҫн•ҙм„ң ліҙм—¬мЈјкі  мһҲлӢӨ.
